title: [InstCombine] Fold select of intrinsic into intrinsic of select
author: xaerru
description:
Fix https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/140917

Transform:
`select cond, intrinsic(x, ...), intrinsic(y, ...)`
into:
`intrinsic(select cond, x, y, ...)`
for `ctpop/ctlz/cttz/abs`.

alive2 proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/Zp55Dy

tests.test_body:
declare void @use(i32)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.ctlz.i32(i32, i1 immarg) #0

define i32 @select_of_ctlz_negative_multi_use(i1 %cond, i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %ctlz1 = call i32 @llvm.ctlz.i32(i32 %x, i1 false)
  call void @use(i32 %ctlz1)
  %ctlz2 = call i32 @llvm.ctlz.i32(i32 %y, i1 false)
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %ctlz1, i32 %ctlz2
  ret i32 %sel
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

declare void @use(i32)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32) #0

define i32 @select_of_ctpop_negative_multi_use(i1 %cond, i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %ctpop1 = call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %x)
  call void @use(i32 %ctpop1)
  %ctpop2 = call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %y)
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %ctpop1, i32 %ctpop2
  ret i32 %sel
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body: @xaerru Can you please update your email settings? See https://llvm.org/docs/DeveloperPolicy.html#email-addresses Then I will merge this PR :)

comments.author: xaerru
comments.body: We can't generalize this to all intrinsics, the intrinsic should be side effect free. We can try to find more intrinsics for which this would work.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> We can't generalize this to all intrinsics, the intrinsic should be side effect free. We can try to find more intrinsics for which this would work.

Yes, of course. What I was referring to was to not hardcode a list of intrinsics and instead do this based on generic queries (like mayHaveSideEffects).

Though seeing as this has no diff on llvm-opt-benchmark, maybe it's not worth pursuing.

Your changes will be combined with recent changes from other authors, then tested by our [build bots](https://lab.llvm.org/buildbot/). If there is a problem with a build, you may receive a report in an email or a comment on this PR.

Please check whether problems have been caused by your change specifically, as the builds can include changes from many authors. It is not uncommon for your change to be included in a build that fails due to someone else's changes, or infrastructure issues.

How to do this, and the rest of the post-merge process, is covered in detail [here](https://llvm.org/docs/MyFirstTypoFix.html#myfirsttypofix-issues-after-landing-your-pr).

If your change does cause a problem, it may be reverted, or you can revert it yourself. This is a normal part of [LLVM development](https://llvm.org/docs/DeveloperPolicy.html#patch-reversion-policy). You can fix your changes and open a new PR to merge them again.

If you don't get any reports, no action is required from you. Your changes are working as expected, well done!
